claims.claimText: The speaker gained 30 pounds during recovery from alcoholism
claims.postExcerpt: I gained 30 pounds in recovery from alcoholism, turning to sugar. then I relapsed & was at my lowest. my doctor prescribed a GLP-1. it’s been a month & I have no desire to drink or binge eat—& thus have more time & energy to do healthy things like exercise. it’s been a miracle.

claims.claimText: A new GLP-1 receptor agonist meta-analysis compared lean mass losses in GLP-1RA trials with lean mass losses in trials where participants lost weight by changing their behavior
claims.postExcerpt: A new GLP-1RA meta-analysis just came out. It compared lean mass losses in trials using GLP-1RAs to trials where people lost weight by changing their behaviors. GLP-1RAs caused the exact same amount of muscle loss as lifestyle-only weight loss, as expected if it's identical.

claims.claimText: The meta-analysis found that GLP-1 receptor agonists caused the same amount of muscle loss as lifestyle-only weight loss
claims.postExcerpt: A new GLP-1RA meta-analysis just came out. It compared lean mass losses in trials using GLP-1RAs to trials where people lost weight by changing their behaviors. GLP-1RAs caused the exact same amount of muscle loss as lifestyle-only weight loss, as expected if it's identical.

wikipediaRefs.title: GLP-1 receptor agonist
wikipediaRefs.snippet: GLP-1 receptor agonists are described as medications used for type 2 diabetes and obesity, among other clinical contexts.

wikipediaRefs.title: Medical doctor
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Defines a medical doctor/physician as a health professional who practices medicine, including providing treatment for physical and mental conditions.

wikipediaRefs.title: Weight loss
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Defines weight loss as reduction of total body mass that can involve loss of fluid, body fat, or lean mass (including muscle/tendon).

wikipediaRefs.title: Semaglutide
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Semaglutide is an anti-diabetic and anti-obesity medication; used for long-term weight management and is sold as Ozempic (diabetes) and Wegovy (weight management).

wikipediaRefs.title: Pet industry
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Describes what the pet industry covers and breaks pet expenditures into categories such as nutrition, supplies/medicines, veterinary care, and other services.

wikipediaRefs.title: Glucagon-like peptide-1
wikipediaRefs.snippet: GLP-1 is an incretin involved in appetite/satiety and GLP-1 receptor agonists gained approval for diabetes/obesity starting in the 2000s; GLP-1-based treatment can be associated with gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea.

wikipediaRefs.title: Naresh Trehan
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Identifies Naresh Trehan as an Indian cardiovascular/cardiothoracic surgeon and chairman/managing director and chief cardiac surgeon of Medanta-The Medicity.

wikipediaRefs.title: Drugs Controller General of India
wikipediaRefs.snippet: DCGI described as head of CDSCO under the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare; responsible for licensing/standards for manufacturing, sales, import and distribution of drugs in India.

wikipediaRefs.title: Placebo
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Explains that placebos are used in randomized clinical trials and ideally kept indistinguishable so participants don’t know assignment.

wikipediaRefs.title: GLP-1 receptor agonist
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Describes GLP-1 agonists as a class used for type 2 diabetes/obesity and notes common adverse effects are gastrointestinal (e.g., nausea, vomiting, diarrhea). Also states FDA requires a boxed warning for thyroid C-cell tumors including medullary thyroid cancer.

wikipediaRefs.title: Semaglutide
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Semaglutide is described as an anti-diabetic medication and an anti-obesity medication (Ozempic/Wegovy/Rybelsus).

wikipediaRefs.title: Drugs Controller General of India
wikipediaRefs.snippet: DCGI is described as head of CDSCO responsible for approval/licensing and setting standards for manufacturing, sales, import, and distribution of drugs in India.

wikipediaRefs.title: Ambrish Mithal
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Background on Dr. Ambrish Mithal as an Indian endocrinologist/diabetologist; chairman/head of Endocrinology and Diabetes at Max Health Care.

wikipediaRefs.title: Max Healthcare
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Max Healthcare Institute Limited is an Indian private hospital chain headquartered in Delhi (founded 2001).

wikipediaRefs.title: Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation
wikipediaRefs.snippet: Provides background on CDSCO as India’s national regulatory body and notes DCGI’s role within CDSCO and its functions (inspections, surveillance, recalls).

wikipediaRefs.title: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
wikipediaRefs.snippet: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) is a Government of India ministry charged with health policy in India.
